search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae shoulder pain telehealth guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae shoulder pain guideline Abu Dhabi
site:bess.ac.uk shoulder pain diagnosis treatment referral guidelines PDF
site:boa.ac.uk shoulder pain guideline primary care
candidate_sources_rejected: DHA muscle sprains and strains guideline as a complete shoulder source
rejection_reasons: The DHA source is not shoulder-specific enough for the broad examination set.
The current BESS summary supersedes the older narrow commissioning guide for this mapping.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: adult common shoulder conditions only; acute fractures, infection, malignancy, and all diagnosis-specific tests require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: Partial: UK primary, community, and intermediate care; not validated for UAE referral or imaging pathways.
UAE_applicability: No direct UAE applicability is established; UAE sources were searched and this specialty-society guidance requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Official BESS PDF and 2021 publication metadata were opened on 2026-07-12; no newer official replacement was identified.
superseded_check: The 2021 BESS summary remains the current official BESS primary/intermediate guideline download identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped infection, trauma, dislocation, cuff-tear, malignancy, inflammatory, and severe-function concern prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped shoulder and rotator-cuff terminology and pain, movement, weakness, trauma, function, and range-of-motion history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports rotator-cuff or impingement terminology and history covering hand dominance, occupation, activity, pain location, radiation, onset, duration, aggravating factors, trauma, comorbidities, red flags, and passive external rotation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped selected imaging and clinician-arranged referral or follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports selected two-view radiography, states ultrasound or MRI is rarely indicated without an agreed specialist pathway, and links persistent symptoms to clinician-arranged referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped stiffness, instability, trauma, range, external rotation, and recurrent-dislocation contexts across common shoulder presentations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports range-of-motion, stiffness, function, instability, trauma, recurrent dislocation, and clinician-selected imaging or referral documentation across common shoulder presentations.

unresolved_source_gaps: Neer, Hawkins-Kennedy, drop-arm, apprehension, Jobe, lift-off, and other individual test accuracy are not established by the reviewed sections.
UAE imaging/referral pathways, antibiotics, injections, analgesia, heat/ice, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
The rotator-cuff-injury diagnosis label remains clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
